title: KeepAlive
content:
### KeepAlive Component

`<KeepAlive>` preserves a component's state when switching between dynamic components:

```vue
<KeepAlive>
  <component :is="currentTab" />
</KeepAlive>
```

Without KeepAlive, the component is destroyed when switched away. With KeepAlive, it stays alive in memory.

### Include and Exclude

Control which components are cached using `include` or `exclude` with component names:

```vue
<KeepAlive include="TabA,TabB">
  <component :is="currentTab" />
</KeepAlive>

<!-- Regex or function -->
<KeepAlive :include="/Tab|Panel/">
  <component :is="currentTab" />
</KeepAlive>
```

### Max

Limit the number of cached instances:

```vue
<KeepAlive :max="10">
  <component :is="currentTab" />
</KeepAlive>
```

When the limit is exceeded, the least recently accessed instance is destroyed.

### Lifecycle Hooks with KeepAlive

Cached components can use `activated` and `deactivated` hooks:

```vue
<script>
export default {
  activated() {
    console.log('Component was re-activated')
  },
  deactivated() {
    console.log('Component was deactivated (cached)')
  }
}
</script>
```

These fire every time the component is switched to/from, unlike `mounted`/`unmounted` which fire only once.

### Cache Invalidation

To manually invalidate a cache, change the `key` attribute on the inner component or use `include`/`exclude` dynamically.

quiz.question: What does the KeepAlive component do?
options: Preserves component state when switching away

quiz.question: Which lifecycle hooks are available for KeepAlive-cached components?
